title: Allotment and Remittances of Wages. The seafarer is required to make
content: 55 an allotment which shall be payable once a month to the seafarer's designated allottee in 56 the Philippines through any authorized Philippine bank. 57 58 The employer shall provide the seafarer with facilities for the seafarer’s designated 59 allottee/s at no expense to the seafarer. The allotment shall be at least eighty percent 60 42 (80%) of the seafarer's monthly salary. The monthly salary shall consist of basic wage 1 plus fixed or guaranteed overtime. 2 3 The employer may also provide facilities for the seafarer to remit any amount earned in 4 excess of the seafarer's allotment, including backwages, if any, to the seafarer's 5 designated allottee/s in the Philippines through any authorized Philippine bank. 6 7 The allotments shall be paid to the designated allottee/s in Philippine currency at the rate 8 of exchange at the time of remittance indicated in the credit advice of the local authorized 9 Philippine bank, which information shall be provided to the designated allottee/s. 10 11 Where a seafarer is held captive on or off the ship as a result of acts of piracy, armed 12 robbery, acts of aggression, war-related violence, terrorism, or other acts of violence 13 against ships or its crew, or any form of detention of the ship, the wages and other 14 entitlements under the seafarer’s employment agreement, SEC or relevant CBA, 15 including the remittance of any allotments, shall continue to be paid during the entire 16 period of captivity and until the seafarer is released and duly repatriated at the point of 17 hire, or where the seafarer dies while in captivity, until the date of death as determined 18 under Philippine law. 19 20
